title: Public-Facing Payment Web Tier Patch and Library Drift

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
spl:
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:container" earliest=-7d
| where match(image_repo,"(?i)checkout|payment|gw|token")
| where match(_raw,"(?i)log4j|openssl|struts|spring4shell)")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by image_repo, image_tag, dq
| sort image_repo, image_tag
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
description: Surfaces outdated frameworks (Log4j, OpenSSL) reported by container image scanners for checkout services, tying technical debt to protection of public-facing web applications.

grandmaExplanation: Surfaces outdated frameworks (Log4j, OpenSSL) reported by container image scanners for checkout services, tying technical debt to protection of public-facing web applications — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Ingest CI-generated image tags with digest. (2) Map repo names to PCI asset IDs. (3) Auto-create patch stories under SLA. (4) Document accepted risk for deferred base image updates.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `qualys:container` or `tenable:sc:vuln` container plugins.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest CI-generated image tags with digest. (2) Map repo names to PCI asset IDs. (3) Auto-create patch stories under SLA. (4) Document accepted risk for deferred base image updates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:container" earliest=-7d
| where match(image_repo,"(?i)checkout|payment|gw|token")
| where match(_raw,"(?i)log4j|openssl|struts|spring4shell)")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by image_repo, image_tag, dq
| sort image_repo, image_tag
```

Understanding this SPL

**Public-Facing Payment Web Tier Patch and Library Drift (PCI DSS Req 6.3.3, 6.2.4)** — Surfaces outdated frameworks (Log4j, OpenSSL) reported by container image scanners for checkout services, tying technical debt to protection of public-facing web applications.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:container` or `tenable:sc:vuln` container plugins. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm; **sourcetype**: qualys:container. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, sourcetype="qualys:container", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(image_repo,"(?i)checkout|payment|gw|token")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"(?i)log4j|openssl|struts|spring4shell)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by image_repo, image_tag, dq** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Public-Facing Payment Web Tier Patch and Library Drift (PCI DSS Req 6.3.3, 6.2.4)** — Surfaces outdated frameworks (Log4j, OpenSSL) reported by container image scanners for checkout services, tying technical debt to protection of public-facing web applications.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:container` or `tenable:sc:vuln` container plugins. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (image_repo, tag, dq), single value (images with findings), timeline (last_seen).
visualization: Table (image_repo, tag, dq), single value (images with findings), timeline (last_seen).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
